IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority in England
TO exercise its functions with a view to securing that it identifies—
(a) all the children and young people in its area who have or may have special educational needs, and
(b) all the children and young people in its area who have a disability.

input:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR an integrated care board, NHS trust or NHS foundation trust
TO (a) inform the child's parent of their opinion and of their duty under subsection (3), and
(b) give the child's parent an opportunity to discuss their opinion with an officer of the board or trust.
WHEN/IF/WHERE, in the course of exercising functions in relation to a child who is under compulsory school age, the board or trust form the opinion that the child has (or probably has) special educational needs or a disability.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR an integrated care board, NHS trust or NHS foundation trust
TO bring their opinion that the child has (or probably has) special educational needs or a disability to the attention of the appropriate local authority in England
WHEN/IF/WHERE, in the course of exercising functions in relation to a child who is under compulsory school age, the board or trust form the opinion that the child has (or probably has) special educational needs or a disability.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR an integrated care board, NHS trust or NHS foundation trust
TO inform the child's parent of a particular voluntary organisation likely to be able to give advice or assistance in connection with any special educational needs or disability the child may have
WHEN/IF/WHERE, in the course of exercising functions in relation to a child who is under compulsory school age, the board or trust form the opinion that the child has (or probably has) special educational needs or a disability.
WHEN/IF/WHERE the board or trust think a particular voluntary organisation is likely to be able to give the parent that.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority in England
TO exercise its functions under this Part with a view to ensuring the integration of educational provision and training provision with health care provision and social care provision
WHEN/IF/WHERE it thinks that this would—
(a) promote the well-being of children or young people in its area who have special educational needs or a disability, or
(b) improve the quality of special educational provision—
(i) made in its area for children or young people who have special educational needs, or
(ii) made outside its area for children or young people for whom it is responsible who have special educational needs.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority in England and its partner commissioning bodies
TO make arrangements (“joint commissioning arrangements”) about the education, health and care provision to be secured for—
(a) children and young people for whom the authority is responsible who have special educational needs, and
(b) children and young people in the authority's area who have a disability.

  "to": "make arrangements (“joint commissioning arrangements”) about the education, health and care provision to be secured for— (a) children and young people for whom the authority is responsible who have special educational needs, and (b) children and young people in the authority's area who have a disability.",

input:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO secure that the EHC plan provides for the child or young person to be educated in a maintained nursery school, mainstream school or mainstream post-16 institution
WHEN/IF/WHERE dealing with a case within section 39(5) or 40(2)
UNLESS  that is incompatible with—
(a) the wishes of the child's parent or the young person, or
(b) the provision of efficient education for others.

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a local authority
TO rely on the exception in subsection (2)(b) in relation to maintained nursery schools, mainstream schools or mainstream post-16 institutions in its area taken as a whole
ONLY IF it shows that there are no reasonable steps that it could take to prevent the incompatibility.

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a local authority
TO rely on the exception in subsection (2)(b) in relation to a particular maintained nursery school, mainstream school or mainstream post-16 institution
ONLY IF it shows that there are no reasonable steps that it or the governing body, proprietor or principal could take to prevent the incompatibility.

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR The governing body, proprietor or principal of a maintained nursery school, mainstream school or mainstream post-16 institution
TO rely on the exception in subsection (2)(b) 
ONLY IF they show that there are no reasonable steps that they or the local authority could take to prevent the incompatibility.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR The child or young person
TO be educated in a maintained nursery school, mainstream school or mainstream post-16 institution
SUBJECT TO subsection (3) or (4)

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR The child or young person
TO be educated in an independent school, a non-maintained special school or a special post-16 institution
WHEN/IF/WHERE the cost is not to be met by a local authority or the Secretary of State

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a child or young person in England who has special educational needs but for whom no EHC plan is maintained
TO be educated in a special school or special post-16 institution during any period in which any of subsections (5) to (9) applies.
output:
{
  "main_section": "https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2014/6/section/34",
  "type": "PERMITTED",
  "for": "a child or young person in England who has special educational needs but for whom no EHC plan is maintained",
  "to": "be educated in a special school or special post-16 institution during any period in which any of subsections (5) to (9) applies.",

TO determine whether it may be necessary for special educational provision to be made for the child or young person in accordance with an EHC plan

IT IS OBLIGATORY

TO consult the child’s parent or the young person

(a) of the reasons for that determination, and
(b) that accordingly it has decided not to secure an EHC needs assessment for the child or young person
WHEN/IF/WHERE the local authority determines that it is not necessary for special educational provision to be made for the child or young person in accordance with an EHC plan.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the local authority
TO notify the child's parent or the young person—
(a) that it is considering securing an EHC needs assessment for the child or young person, and
(b) that the parent or young person has the right to—
(i) express views to the authority (orally or in writing), and
(ii) submit evidence to the authority.

IT IS OBLIGATORY

TO secure an EHC needs assessment for the child or young person
WHEN/IF/WHERE, after considering views and evidence, it is of the opinion that the child or young person has or may have special educational needs and that it may be necessary for special educational provision to be made in accordance with an EHC plan

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority in England
TO notify the child's parent or the young person of—
(a) the outcome of the assessment,
(b) whether it proposes to secure that an EHC plan is prepared for the child or young person, and
(c) the reasons for that decision
AFTER an EHC needs assessment has been carried out.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO consider whether a young person aged over 18 requires additional time, in comparison to the majority of others of the same age who do not have special educational needs, to complete his or her education or training
WHEN/IF/WHERE making a determination or forming an opinion for the purposes of this section in relation to he or she.

input:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO consult—
(a) the governing body, proprietor or principal of the school or other institution,
(b) the governing body, proprietor or principal of any other school or other institution the authority is considering having named in the plan, and
(c) if a school or other institution is within paragraph (a) or (b) and is maintained by another local authority, that authority.
WHEN/IF/WHERE, before the end of the period specified in a notice under section 38(2)(b), a request is made to a local authority to secure that a particular school or other institution is named in an EHC plan.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO secure that the EHC plan names the school or other institution specified in the request, unless subsection (4) applies
WHEN/IF/WHERE, before the end of the period specified in a notice under section 38(2)(b), a request is made to a local authority to secure that a particular school or other institution is named in an EHC plan.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO secure that the plan—
(a) names a school or other institution which the local authority thinks would be appropriate for the child or young person, or
(b) specifies the type of school or other institution which the local authority thinks would be appropriate for the child or young person
WHEN/IF/WHERE, before the end of the period specified in a notice under section 38(2)(b), a request is made to a local authority to secure that a particular school or other institution is named in an EHC plan.
WHEN/IF/WHERE (a) the school or other institution requested is unsuitable for the age, ability, aptitude or special educational needs of the child or young person concerned, or
(b) the attendance of the child or young person at the requested school or other institution would be incompatible with—
(i) the provision of efficient education for others, or
(ii) the efficient use of resources.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO consult—
(a) the governing body, proprietor or principal of any school or other institution the authority is considering having named in the plan, and
(b) if that school or other institution is maintained by another local authority, that authority
WHEN/IF/WHERE, before the end of the period specified in a notice under section 38(2)(b), a request is made to a local authority to secure that a particular school or other institution is named in an EHC plan
WHEN/IF/WHERE it has not already done so
BEFORE securing that the plan names a school or other institution under subsection (5)(a).

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO, at the end of the period specified in the notice under section 38(2)(b), secure that any changes it thinks necessary are made to the draft EHC plan
WHEN/IF/WHERE, before the end of the period specified in a notice under section 38(2)(b), a request is made to a local authority to secure that a particular school or other institution is named in an EHC plan.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO send a copy of the finalised EHC plan to—
(a) the child's parent or the young person, and
(b) the governing body, proprietor or principal of any school or other institution named in the plan.

input:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the local authority
TO secure the specified special educational provision for the child or young person
UNLESS the child's parent or the young person has made suitable alternative arrangements.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the responsible commissioning body
TO arrange the specified health care provision for the child or young person.
WHEN/IF/WHERE the plan specifies health care provision.
UNLESS the child's parent or the young person has made suitable alternative arrangements.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO review an EHC plan that it maintains—
(a) in the period of 12 months starting with the date on which the plan was first made, and
(b) in each subsequent period of 12 months starting with the date on which the plan was last reviewed under this section
SUBJECT TO any contrary provision in regulations made under subsection (7)(b).

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO secure a re-assessment of the educational, health care and social care needs of a child or young person for whom it maintains an EHC plan
WHEN/IF/WHERE a request is made to it by—
(a) the child's parent or the young person, or
(b) the governing body, proprietor or principal of the school, post-16 institution or other institution which the child or young person attends
SUBJECT TO any contrary provision in regulations made under subsection (7)(b).

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a local authority
TO secure a re-assessment of those needs
WHEN/IF/WHERE it thinks it necessary.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO have regard to whether the educational or training outcomes specified in the plan have been achieved
WHEN/IF/WHERE reviewing an EHC plan maintained for a young person aged over 18, or deciding whether to secure a re-assessment of the needs of such a young person.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO consult the parent of the child, or the young person, for whom it maintains the EHC plan
WHEN/IF/WHERE conducting a review or re-assessment.

input:
IT IS PROHIBITED
FOR a person
TO fails to comply with any requirement—
(a) in respect of the discovery or inspection of documents, or
(b) to attend to give evidence and produce documents,
WHEN/IF/WHERE that requirement is imposed by Tribunal Procedure Rules in relation to an appeal under this section or regulations under subsection (4)(a)
UNLESS the person has some reasonable excuse.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO notify the child's parent or the young person of—
(a) the right to mediation under section 53 or 54, and
(b) the requirement to obtain a certificate under section 55 before making certain appeals.
BEFORE the end of the prescribed period after the decision is made, or the plan is made, amended or replaced,

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the parent or young person
TO inform the local authority
WHEN/IF/WHERE he or she wishes to pursue mediation under section 53 or 54, of—
(a) that fact, and
(b) the issues in respect of which he or she wishes to pursue mediation (“the mediation issues”).

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the parent or young person
TO inform the local authority of the health care provision which he or she wishes to be specified in the plan
WHEN/IF/WHERE the mediation issues are, or include, the fact that no health care provision, or no health care provision of a particular kind, is specified in the plan.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the local authority
TO notify each relevant commissioning body of—
(a) the mediation issues, and
(b) anything of which it has been informed by the parent or young person under section 52(4).

input:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the responsible commissioning body (or, where there is more than one, the responsible commissioning bodies acting jointly)
TO —
(a) arrange for mediation between it (or them) and the parent or young person,
(b) ensure that the mediation is conducted by an independent person, and
(c) participate in the mediation
WHEN/IF/WHERE the mediation issues are limited to the health care provision specified in the plan or the fact that no health care provision, or no health care provision of a particular kind, is specified in the plan.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the local authority
TO —
(i) arrange for mediation between it, each responsible commissioning body and the parent or young person,
(ii) ensure that the mediation is conducted by an independent person, and
(iii) participate in the mediation
WHEN/IF/WHERE the mediation issues include anything else.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR each responsible commissioning body
TO also participate in the mediation
WHEN/IF/WHERE the mediation issues include anything else.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the local authority
TO —
(a) arrange for mediation between it and the parent or young person,
(b) ensure that the mediation is conducted by an independent person, and
(c) participate in the mediation.
WHEN/IF/WHERE—
(a) the parent or young person informs the local authority under section 52 that he or she wishes to pursue mediation, and
(b) the mediation issues do not include health care provision specified in the plan or the fact that no health care provision, or no health care provision of a particular kind, is specified in the plan.
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR The parent or young person may make the appeal
ONLY IF a mediation adviser has issued a certificate to him or her under subsection (4) or (5)
WHEN/IF/WHERE a child's parent or young person intends to appeal to the First-tier Tribunal under section 51 or regulations made under that section in respect of—
(a) a decision of a local authority, or
(b) the content of an EHC plan maintained by a local authority
UNLESS the appeal concerns only—
(a) the school or other institution named in an EHC plan;
(b) the type of school or other institution specified in an EHC plan;
(c) the fact that an EHC plan does not name a school or other institution.

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR the parent or young person
TO make the appeal
ONLY IF a mediation adviser has issued a certificate to him or her under subsection (4) or (5)
WHEN/IF/WHERE a child's parent or young person intends to appeal to the First-tier Tribunal under section 51 or regulations made under that section in respect of—
(a) a decision of a local authority, or
(b) the content of an EHC plan maintained by a local authority
UNLESS the appeal concerns only—
(a) the school or other institution named in an EHC plan;
(b) the type of school or other institution specified in an EHC plan;
(c) the fact that an EHC plan does not name a school or other institution.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a mediation adviser
TO issue a certificate under this subsection to the parent or young person
WHEN/IF/WHERE—
(a) the adviser has provided him or her with information and advice about pursuing mediation under section 53 or 54, and
(b) the parent or young person has informed the adviser that he or she does not wish to pursue mediation.
WHEN/IF/WHERE a child's parent or young person intends to appeal to the First-tier Tribunal under section 51 or regulations made under that section in respect of—
(a) a decision of a local authority, or
(b) the content of an EHC plan maintained by a local authority
UNLESS the appeal concerns only—
(a) the school or other institution named in an EHC plan;
(b) the type of school or other institution specified in an EHC plan;
(c) the fact that an EHC plan does not name a school or other institution.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a mediation adviser
TO issue a certificate under this subsection to the parent or young person
WHEN/IF/WHERE the adviser has provided him or her with information and advice about pursuing mediation under section 53 or 54, and the parent or young person has—
(a) informed the adviser that he or she wishes to pursue mediation under the appropriate section, and
(b) participated in such mediation.
WHEN/IF/WHERE a child's parent or young person intends to appeal to the First-tier Tribunal under section 51 or regulations made under that section in respect of—
(a) a decision of a local authority, or
(b) the content of an EHC plan maintained by a local authority
UNLESS the appeal concerns only—
(a) the school or other institution named in an EHC plan;
(b) the type of school or other institution specified in an EHC plan;
(c) the fact that an EHC plan does not name a school or other institution.

input:
IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a local authority in England
TO do so
ONLY IF satisfied that it would be inappropriate for the provision to be made in a school or post-16 institution or at such a place.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR a local authority
TO consult the child's parent or the young person
BEFORE doing so.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the local authority
TO pay any fees payable in respect of education or training provided for the child or young person at that school, institution or place in accordance with the EHC plan.
WHEN/IF/WHERE —
(a) a local authority maintains an EHC plan for a child or young person,
(b) special educational provision in respect of the child or young person is made at a school, post-16 institution or place at which relevant early years education is provided, and
(c) that school, institution or place is named in the EHC plan.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the local authority
TO pay any fees payable in respect of the special educational provision made at the school, institution or place in question which is required to meet the special educational needs of the child or young person
WHEN/IF/WHERE —
(a) a local authority is responsible for a child or young person for whom no EHC plan is maintained,
(b) special educational provision in respect of the child or young person is made at a school, post-16 institution or place at which relevant early years education is provided, and
(c) the local authority is satisfied that—
(i) the interests of the child or young person require special educational provision to be made, and
(ii) it is appropriate for education or training to be provided to the child or young person at the school, institution or place in question.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the authority
TO also pay any fees in respect of the board and lodging
WHEN/IF/WHERE board and lodging are provided for the child or young person at the school, post-16 institution or place mentioned in subsection (2) or (4)
WHEN/IF/WHERE satisfied that special educational provision cannot be provided at the school, post-16 institution or place 
UNLESS the board and lodging are also provided.

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR a local authority in England
TO supply goods and services to—
(a) the governing body of a maintained school or maintained nursery school in England;
(b) the proprietor of an Academy;
(c) the governing body of an institution within the further education sector that the authority thinks is or is to be attended by a young person for whom the authority maintains an EHC plan
ONLY IF this is done the purpose set out in subsection (2).

IT IS PERMITTED
FOR the goods and services
TO be supplied on the terms and conditions that the authority thinks fit, including terms as to payment.

input:
IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the home authority
TO determine whether it may be necessary for special educational provision to be made for the detained person in accordance with an EHC plan on release from detention
WHEN/IF/WHERE this subsection applies
WHEN/IF/WHERE —
(a) a request is made under subsection (2),
(b) the detained person has been brought to the home authority's attention by any person as someone who has or may have special educational needs, or
(c) the detained person has otherwise come to the home authority's attention as someone who has or may have special educational needs.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the home authority
TO consult the appropriate person and the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation
WHEN/IF/WHERE making a determination on the necessity of a detained person’s EHC needs assessment.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the home authority
TO notify the appropriate person and the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation where the detained person is detained—
(a) that it is considering securing a detained person's EHC needs assessment for the detained person, and
(b) that the appropriate person and the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation where the detained person is detained each have the right to—
(i) express views to the authority (orally or in writing), and
(ii) submit evidence to the authority
WHEN/IF/WHERE —
(a) the detained person has not been assessed under this section or section 36 during the previous six months, and
(b) the home authority determines that it may be necessary for special educational provision to be made for the detained person in accordance with an EHC plan on release from detention.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the home authority
TO secure a detained person's EHC needs assessment
WHEN/IF/WHERE, after having regard to any views expressed and evidence submitted under subsection (8), the authority is of the opinion that—
(a) the detained person has or may have special educational needs, and
(b) it may be necessary for special educational provision to be made for the detained person in accordance with an EHC plan on release from detention.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the local authority
TO notify the appropriate person and the person in charge of the relevant youth accommodation where the detained person is detained of—
(a) the outcome of the assessment,
(b) whether it proposes to secure that an EHC plan is prepared for the detained person, and
(c) the reasons for that decision
AFTER a detained person's EHC needs assessment has been carried out.

output:

FOR a person
TO fail to comply with any requirement—
(a) in respect of the discovery or inspection of documents, or
(b) to attend to give evidence and produce documents,
WHEN/IF/WHERE that requirement is imposed by Tribunal Procedure Rules in relation to an appeal under this section
UNLESS the person has some reasonable excuse.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the mediation adviser
TO notify the authority
WHEN/IF/WHERE, by virtue of subsection (7), the appropriate person has informed the mediation adviser that he or she wishes to pursue mediation with the home authority.

IT IS OBLIGATORY
FOR the authority
TO —
(i) arrange for mediation between it and the appropriate person,
(ii) ensure that the mediation is conducted by an independent person, and
(iii) participate in the mediation
WHEN/IF/WHERE, by virtue of subsection (7), the appropriate person has informed the mediation adviser that he or she wishes to pursue mediation with the home authority.

(a) children in England with special educational needs, and
(b) young people aged under 19 in England with special educational needs.
